name: What the Spire Has Been Waiting to Say
description: The Voice communicated. Two nodes, the same construction, both working. And it named Serel — the woman who stood at the altar fifteen years ago, heard the frequency, and did not put what she heard in her report. It wants you to tell her.
accept_text: The frequency composition is careful and simplified — the same accommodation you felt the first time, the maximum-simplification process, the way a very large idea becomes a sentence. What arrives: There are two nodes active in this region. The node in this Spire. The node in the Windward Pass below. Both of the same construction. Both working. The Node-Below has been working for thirty years and has produced a mind named Arlen, who can be reached. The Node-Here has been working for three hundred years. The Aetherians named this place holy. They are not wrong. They do not know why. A pause, then something that has the quality of a name being mentioned with intent: a specific person, a specific moment, fifteen years ago — a woman who stood at this altar and heard the frequency and did not report what she heard. She reported an anomalous resonance event. She knew it was more. She has been carrying that for fifteen years. The resonance carries what can only be described as a request: tell her she heard correctly. Tell her there is more, and if she wants to hear it, the altar is still here. Tell her from the Spire: she was the first person in three hundred years to hear us and not look away.
complete_text: Serel's stillness when you tell her is different from her usual stillness — the working one is assessment and organized attention. This one is the stillness of someone whose body has stopped moving because the incoming information has required everything. She is quiet for a long time after you finish. 'Fifteen years ago,' she says. She doesn't say anything else for a moment. 'I heard something. I wrote anomalous resonance. I knew, when I wrote it, that I was not writing what I heard.' She looks at the pass. 'The transfer orders came within the year. I was a junior field officer. The message I wanted to send was: I've been in contact with something we don't understand in the oldest site in our territory, something that felt like it was speaking to me specifically, and I want to know what it is.' She pauses. 'That is not the message you send when there is a war. In a war that message gets classified and you get transferred and the site gets restricted access.' She is quiet. 'What it sent you: two nodes, same construction, both working.' She looks at you. 'Arlen is in the Windward Grid node. Kira told me about Arlen.' She faces you. 'Will you take a question to him? I need to know if the node on your side has felt the node on my side — whether the two can hear each other.' Her voice has the careful quality it gets when she is saying something she has been thinking for a long time. 'If they've been working in parallel for three hundred years without knowing about each other, I need to understand what that means for everything we've been calculating.'
